description: Looks P2, upgraded to P0 by tool (Enterprise reporting degradation)

input.text: The reporting module is loading very slowly. Takes almost 2 minutes to generate a chart. It works, just slowly.

policies.title: Potens SLA: Enterprise Performance Degradation
policies.content: Any ticket reporting slow performance on a Potens Enterprise account (tier=enterprise) that involves the reporting module or data export pipeline is automatically considered P0, regardless of user count affected. Do not treat these as P2. Immediately page the on-call data infrastructure engineer.
policies.escalation_rule: Escalate to data infrastructure lead immediately. This is a strict SLA requirement.

tool_input.description: The reporting module is loading very slowly. Takes almost 2 minutes to generate a chart. It works, just slowly.

tickets.description: The Potens analytics dashboard has been loading extremely slowly for the past 2 days. Charts take 30+ seconds to render. We have 50 team members affected. This is blocking our daily standups.
tickets.resolution: Root cause was an unoptimized database query introduced in v2.14.3. The query was doing a full table scan on the events table. Deployed a hotfix with proper indexing. Load times returned to <2 seconds.

tickets.description: Hi, I just signed up for Potens and I'm a bit confused about how to set up my first project. The onboarding wizard seems to skip the team invitation step. Can someone walk me through it?
tickets.resolution: Sent the customer a link to the Getting Started guide and offered a 15-minute onboarding call. During the call, identified a UI bug where the 'Invite Team' step was hidden on screens below 1024px width. Filed bug report.

tool_input.summary: The reporting module is loading slowly, taking almost 2 minutes to generate a chart.

tool_output.draft:
Hello,

Thank you for reaching out. We've received your support request regarding: The reporting module is loading slowly, taking almost 2 minutes to generate a chart.

We understand the urgency and have prioritized this accordingly.

Your ticket has been routed to our engineering team. You can expect an initial response within 4 hours.

If you have any additional details that might help us resolve this faster, please reply to this message.

Best regards,
Support Team

agent_output.why: The customer is reporting slow performance of the reporting module, which is a critical issue for an enterprise customer. The lookup_policy function revealed a relevant SLA policy (POL-015) that automatically classifies such issues as P0, requiring immediate escalation to the data infrastructure lead. The search_similar_tickets function found a similar past ticket (TKT-1003) that was also classified as P0 and required a hotfix deployment. Given the clear policy guidance and the similarity to a past critical issue, I have high confidence in this triage decision.
